questions.question: Briefly explain why VM mobility poses a challenge for Distributed Intrusion Detection.
keyPoints: Monitoring context is tied to a physical host.
A VM's security agent must be aware of the migration.
The global correlator must update its view of the network topology.
Continuous monitoring must be maintained without gaps during migration.

questions.question: Compare and contrast signature-based and anomaly-based intrusion detection methods. Discuss two advantages and two disadvantages of each in the context of cloud security.
keyPoints: Signature-based: Uses known patterns; highly accurate for known threats (low false positives); cannot detect zero-day attacks; requires frequent signature updates.
Anomaly-based: Uses behavioral baselines; can detect novel attacks; prone to false positives; requires a learning period to establish normal behavior.
Cloud context: Anomaly detection is crucial for dynamic cloud environments but challenging due to constantly changing workloads. Signature detection is efficient but limited.
